name: First-Order Phase Transition (Clapeyron)
description: Two phases reach coexistence when their chemical potentials are equal; the Clapeyron equation then relates the slope of the phase boundary to the entropy and volume changes, and crossing the boundary drives the transition with its latent heat.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Two phases in contact"]
    N2{"Chemical potentials equal?"}
    N3["Phase with lower μ grows"]
    N4["Coexistence: μ1 = μ2"]
    N5["Clapeyron: dP/dT = ΔS/ΔV"]
    N6["Latent heat ΔH = TΔS"]
    N7["Boundary on P–T diagram"]
    N8["Cross boundary -> transition"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 -->|no| N3
    N3 -->|continue| N2
    N2 -->|yes| N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|re-evaluate| N2

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

sources.title: Physical Chemistry, 11th ed. — Physical Transformations of Pure Substances
sources.authors: Atkins, P.; de Paula, J.; Keeler, J.

sources.title: NIST Chemistry WebBook — phase-change data
sources.authors: Linstrom, P. J.; Mallard, W. G. (eds.)

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

nodeDetails.label: Cross boundary -> transition
nodeDetails.detail: Change T or P past the line
